description: An assembly of people for a purpose; the event of coming together. 'Lona' names when paths converge—the moment of shared presence.
sound_symbolism: The word gathers simply. 'Lo' opens with welcoming space, gathering beginning. 'Na' settles with grounded arrival, people present.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'lo lona' (the meeting). Related to 'senura' (council) but less formal. The event of 'sorui' (circle) forming.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Meeting as dialogue—the gathering where voices can be heard and decisions made together.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Meeting as presence—coming together with full attention to one another.
